root_text.an5.142:1.3: Idha, bhikkhave, ekacco puggalo ārabhati ca vippaṭisārī ca hoti;
root_text.an5.142:1.4: tañca cetovimuttiṁ paññāvimuttiṁ yathābhūtaṁ nappajānāti yatthassa te uppannā pāpakā akusalā dhammā aparisesā nirujjhanti.
root_text.an5.142:2.1: Idha pana, bhikkhave, ekacco puggalo ārabhati, na vippaṭisārī hoti;
root_text.an5.142:2.2: tañca cetovimuttiṁ paññāvimuttiṁ yathābhūtaṁ nappajānāti yatthassa te uppannā pāpakā akusalā dhammā aparisesā nirujjhanti.
root_text.an5.142:3.1: Idha pana, bhikkhave, ekacco puggalo na ārabhati, vippaṭisārī hoti;
root_text.an5.142:3.2: tañca cetovimuttiṁ paññāvimuttiṁ yathābhūtaṁ nappajānāti yatthassa te uppannā pāpakā akusalā dhammā aparisesā nirujjhanti.
root_text.an5.142:4.1: Idha pana, bhikkhave, ekacco puggalo na ārabhati na vippaṭisārī hoti;
root_text.an5.142:4.2: tañca cetovimuttiṁ paññāvimuttiṁ yathābhūtaṁ nappajānāti yatthassa te uppannā pāpakā akusalā dhammā aparisesā nirujjhanti.
root_text.an5.142:5.1: Idha pana, bhikkhave, ekacco puggalo na ārabhati na vippaṭisārī hoti;
root_text.an5.142:5.2: tañca cetovimuttiṁ paññāvimuttiṁ yathābhūtaṁ pajānāti yatthassa te uppannā pāpakā akusalā dhammā aparisesā nirujjhanti.
root_text.an5.142:6.1: Tatra, bhikkhave, yvāyaṁ puggalo ārabhati ca vippaṭisārī ca hoti,
root_text.an5.142:6.2: tañca cetovimuttiṁ paññāvimuttiṁ yathābhūtaṁ nappajānāti yatthassa te uppannā pāpakā akusalā dhammā aparisesā nirujjhanti,
root_text.an5.142:6.4: ‘āyasmato kho ārambhajā āsavā saṁvijjanti, vippaṭisārajā āsavā pavaḍḍhanti, sādhu vatāyasmā ārambhaje āsave pahāya vippaṭisāraje āsave paṭivinodetvā cittaṁ paññañca bhāvetu;
root_text.an5.142:6.5: evamāyasmā amunā pañcamena puggalena samasamo bhavissatī’ti.
root_text.an5.142:7.1: Tatra, bhikkhave, yvāyaṁ puggalo ārabhati na vippaṭisārī hoti,
root_text.an5.142:7.2: tañca cetovimuttiṁ paññāvimuttiṁ yathābhūtaṁ nappajānāti yatthassa te uppannā pāpakā akusalā dhammā aparisesā nirujjhanti,
root_text.an5.142:7.4: ‘āyasmato kho ārambhajā āsavā saṁvijjanti, vippaṭisārajā āsavā na pavaḍḍhanti, sādhu vatāyasmā ārambhaje āsave pahāya cittaṁ paññañca bhāvetu;
root_text.an5.142:7.5: evamāyasmā amunā pañcamena puggalena samasamo bhavissatī’ti.
root_text.an5.142:8.1: Tatra, bhikkhave, yvāyaṁ puggalo na ārabhati vippaṭisārī hoti,
root_text.an5.142:8.2: tañca cetovimuttiṁ paññāvimuttiṁ yathābhūtaṁ nappajānāti yatthassa te uppannā pāpakā akusalā dhammā aparisesā nirujjhanti,
root_text.an5.142:8.4: ‘āyasmato kho ārambhajā āsavā na saṁvijjanti, vippaṭisārajā āsavā pavaḍḍhanti, sādhu vatāyasmā vippaṭisāraje āsave paṭivinodetvā cittaṁ paññañca bhāvetu;
root_text.an5.142:8.5: evamāyasmā amunā pañcamena puggalena samasamo bhavissatī’ti.
root_text.an5.142:9.1: Tatra, bhikkhave, yvāyaṁ puggalo na ārabhati na vippaṭisārī hoti,
root_text.an5.142:9.2: tañca cetovimuttiṁ paññāvimuttiṁ yathābhūtaṁ nappajānāti yatthassa te uppannā pāpakā akusalā dhammā aparisesā nirujjhanti,
root_text.an5.142:9.4: ‘āyasmato kho ārambhajā āsavā na saṁvijjanti, vippaṭisārajā āsavā na pavaḍḍhanti, sādhu vatāyasmā cittaṁ paññañca bhāvetu;
root_text.an5.142:9.5: evamāyasmā amunā pañcamena puggalena samasamo bhavissatī’ti.
root_text.an5.142:10.1: Iti kho, bhikkhave, ime cattāro puggalā amunā pañcamena puggalena evaṁ ovadiyamānā evaṁ anusāsiyamānā anupubbena āsavānaṁ khayaṁ pāpuṇantī”ti.

translation_text.an5.142:1.1: “Mendicants, these five individuals are found in the world.
translation_text.an5.142:1.3: One individual transgresses and regrets it.
translation_text.an5.142:1.4: And they don’t truly understand the freedom of heart and freedom by wisdom where those arisen bad, unskillful qualities cease without anything left over.
translation_text.an5.142:2.1: One individual transgresses and doesn’t regret it.
translation_text.an5.142:3.1: One individual doesn’t transgress yet still feels regret.
translation_text.an5.142:4.1: One individual neither transgresses nor regrets.
translation_text.an5.142:5.1: One individual neither transgresses nor regrets.
translation_text.an5.142:5.2: And they do understand where those arisen bad, unskillful qualities cease without anything left over.
translation_text.an5.142:6.1: Take the case of the individual who transgresses and regrets it.
translation_text.an5.142:6.2: And they don’t truly understand the freedom of heart and freedom by wisdom where those arisen bad, unskillful qualities cease without anything left over.
translation_text.an5.142:6.4: ‘Venerable, the defilements born of transgression are found in you, and the defilements born of regret grow. You would do well to give up the defilements born of transgression and get rid of the defilements born of regret, and then develop the mind and wisdom.
translation_text.an5.142:6.5: In this way you’ll become just like the fifth individual.’
translation_text.an5.142:7.1: Take the case of the individual who transgresses and doesn’t regret it.
translation_text.an5.142:7.4: ‘Venerable, the defilements born of transgression are found in you, but the defilements born of regret don’t grow. You would do well to give up the defilements born of transgression, and then develop the mind and wisdom.
translation_text.an5.142:7.5: In this way you’ll become just like the fifth individual.’
translation_text.an5.142:8.1: Take the case of the individual who doesn’t transgress yet feels regret.
translation_text.an5.142:8.4: ‘Venerable, the defilements born of transgression are not found in you, yet the defilements born of regret grow. You would do well to get rid of the defilements born of regret, and then develop the mind and wisdom.
translation_text.an5.142:8.5: In this way you’ll become just like the fifth individual.’
translation_text.an5.142:9.1: Take the case of the individual who neither transgresses nor regrets.
translation_text.an5.142:9.4: ‘Venerable, the defilements born of transgression are not found in you, and the defilements born of regret don’t grow. You would do well to develop the mind and wisdom.
translation_text.an5.142:9.5: In this way you’ll become just like the fifth individual.’
translation_text.an5.142:10.1: And so, mendicants, when these four individuals are advised and instructed by comparison with the fifth individual, they gradually attain the ending of defilements.”

variant_text.an5.142:6.4: ārambhajā → ārabhajā (sya-all, km); ārabbhajā (pts1ed, mr) | pavaḍḍhanti → saṁvaḍḍhanti (mr)
